One additional reason supporting this is that the subscapularis functions primarily in internal rotation and adduction of the arm, which aligns more closely with its attachment site at the lesser tubercle, contrasting with the externally rotating actions facilitated by the other three muscles connected to the greater tubercle.

My final answer is: $\boxed{D}$.
